description_long: This is a bulbous, wide-bodied ceramic vase with a narrow, cylindrical neck and a slightly flared rim. The surface is adorned with intricate, incised spiral and geometric line patterns, primarily in white, which contrast against the earthy brown and gray tones of the clay. The body of the vase is rounded and squat, giving it a stable base, while the neck rises vertically and is decorated with angular, stepped motifs. The finish appears matte and slightly rough, suggesting it is handmade or aged. The interior is visible at the mouth, indicating it is hollow and capable of holding contents. Subtle discolorations and surface marks hint at age or use, adding to its character.
description: A wide, rounded ceramic vase with a narrow neck, decorated with white spiral and geometric incised patterns on a matte, earthy surface.
description_view_0: The vase's broadest side is visible, showcasing the spiral incised pattern radiating from the lower left and the stepped motif on the neck.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the continuation of the spiral pattern and a clearer view of the stepped geometric design on the neck.
description_view_2: The front of the vase displays the central spiral motif and the most prominent stepped pattern on the neck, with subtle discoloration near the rim.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the symmetry of the spiral lines and the evenness of the neck's geometric decoration, with the rim appearing slightly uneven.
